# 1. Introduction


Inorganic nanomaterials possessing enzyme-like activities, such as peroxidase-like activity, oxidase-like and SOD/catalaselike activity, are called nanozymes. In contrast with natural enzymes, nanozymes have remarkable merits, such as easy preparation, low-cost, controllable catalytic activity and stable against harsh conditions, thus possessing a promising future in enzyme-related applications. Since ferromagnetic nanoparticles (NPs) were excitingly discovered as a peroxidase mimetic for the  rst time, 1large amounts of nanozymes have emerged in the following years, and they have been used as effective colorimetric tools for biosensing, 2immunoassays, 3environmental monitoring4and so on. Even now they are still a hot research area. However, the majority of these analysis methods are based on the peroxidase mimetic properties of nanomaterials, which could catalyze the oxidation of typical chromogenic substrates using H2O2as an oxidant, while the addition of high concentrations of H2O2would make the application difficult because of the unstable and destructive H2O2. Thus, it was necessary to develop novel enzyme mimetics for better application.

Oxidase enzymes that can catalyze the oxidation of substrates by simply employing molecular oxygen as the

# Evaluation of the antioxidant activity of phenols and tannic acid determination with Mn3O4nanooctahedrons as an oxidase mimic †


Xiaodan Zhang and Yuming Huang*

In this paper, we successfully synthesized novel Mn3O4nano-octahedrons using a simple hydrothermal method and demonstrated that the prepared Mn3O4nanoparticles (NPs) exhibited highly intrinsic oxidase-like activity and could catalyze the reaction of TMB, ABTS and OPD to produce a typical color reaction in the absence of H2O2within minutes. Compared with other non-noble metal NPs oxidase nanozymes, the affinity of Mn3O4NPs to TMB was increased by at least35% according to the Km values. It was deduced that O2c /C0and few OH c radicals were produced during the catalytic reaction. Then, the addition of twelve phenols to the TMB -Mn3O4NP system led to the fading of the solution by different degrees based on the antioxidant activity of the phenols. Thus, preliminary assessment of the antioxidant ability of twelve phenols using TMB decolorization assays was realized based on the excellent oxidaselike properties of the Mn3O4NPs. Finally, under the optimum conditions, visual determination of tannic acid (TA) was realized and the detection limit was19nM with a linear range from0.05to1.4m M. The proposed method was successfully used to determine TA in tea samples.

electron acceptor are more facile, economic and environmental. Actually, some peroxidase nanozymes also exhibit oxidase-like properties such as CeO2NPs, 5Au@Pt nanostructures, 6MnO2nanomaterials, 7Fe -Co NPs, 8CoFe2O4NPs9and polyoxometalate (POM) NPs. 10Nevertheless, almost all of the analytical applications of these nanozymes were only focused on their peroxidase-like catalytic activities, while their oxidaselike enzyme character was o  en overlooked. Only a few attempts have been made to investigate the analytical applications based on the oxidase mimetic activities of nanomaterials. For example, investigators have found that naked3.6nm Au NPs exhibited glucose oxidase-like activity, and were capable of catalyzing the aerobic oxidation of glucose to gluconate under mild conditions. 11The oxidase mimic of Au@Pt nanorods has been used as a screening tool for enzyme inhibitors, and the selective and quantitative detection of Hg2+ ions was realized. 12Recently, Hu et al. fabricated three-dimensional (3D) nanoassemblies of noble metal Au/Ag NPs as an oxidase mimic for catalyzing the degradation of methyl blue (MB). 13However, all of these analytical applications based on oxidase mimics were prepared using noble metals with a complicated preparation method which was time-consuming and not economical. So it is necessary to develop oxidase mimics of non-noble metal nanomaterials because related work is scarce. For instance, polymer-coated CeO2NPs, MnO2nanowires and folate-functionalized POMs have been employed as oxidase-like biocatalysts for immunosorbent assays14and an increasing focus on POMs has emerged because of the excellent catalytic properties of POMs; 15metal oxide NPs of CoFe2O4were found to

possess oxidase-like activity in our earlier investigation and were used to determine sul  te based on luminol chemiluminescence. 16

In recent years, manganese oxides have been of extensive interest in many applications due to their outstanding structural  exibility combined with novel physical and chemical properties. Among the studied manganese oxides, Mn3O4possesses many excellent properties due to its unique mixedvalance state, high activity, durability, and low cost. For example, as an effective catalyst for oxidation and reduction reactions, Mn3O4has played the role of catalyst for the oxidation of methane, 17phenolic contaminants18and the degradation of dyes, 19or the reduction of nitrobenzene. 20But to our knowledge, the investigation of the enzyme-like activity of Mn3O4NPs has not been reported.

In this study, we investigated the potential enzyme mimic activity of Mn3O4NPs. As expected, it was found that Mn3O4NPs could effectively catalyze the reaction of organic substrates [3,3,5,5-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB), o -phenylenediamine (OPD) and2,2 0 -azino-bis(3-ethylbenzothiazoline-6-sulfonic acid) diammonium salt (ABTS)] to produce the typical colors in the absence of H2O2. Then, we utilized the noncarcinogenic TMB system as a model enzyme-like reaction to investigate the oxidase-like catalytic property of Mn3O4NPs. Furthermore, we preliminarily evaluated the antioxidant ability of twelve phenols based on the possible mechanism that radicals are produced during the TMB colorimetric reaction. Finally, the antioxidant tendencies of phenols in the results with the TMB -Mn3O4NP system showed similar trends with the Folin -Ciocalteu (F -C) method, which is regarded as a measurement of overall antioxidant capacity rather than phenolic content suggested by investigators. 21Thus, we proposed a method for preliminarily assessing the antioxidant ability of twelve phenols using TMB decolorization assays. As an analytical method,  nally, tannic acid (TA) was used as an example to prove the real application of the TMB -Mn3O4NP system with satisfactory results.


# 2. Experimental



# 2.1Reagents


All the reagents used in the experiments were analytical grade and used without further puri  cation. Potassium permanganate (KMnO4) was purchased from Tianjin Guangfu Fine Chemical Research Institute (Tianjin, China), and TMB was purchased from Sigma-Aldrich and stored in a refrigerator at4 /C14C. Disodium ethylenediaminetetraacetate dihydrate (EDTA2Na $ 2H2O), tannic acid, gallic acid, pyrogallol, catechol, m -dihydroxybenzene, hydroquinone, phloroglucin, 2,4-dichlorophenol, pentachlorophenol, p -nitrophenol, 2,4,6-trinitrophenol, and phenol were all of analytical grade and purchased from Chongqing Chemical Reagents Company (Chongqing, China). The working solutions were prepared fresh daily and diluted using ultra-pure water for further use. Ultrapure water was used in all experiments. All glassware was soaked in10% nitric acid and thoroughly cleaned before use.


# 2.2Preparation of the Mn3O4NPs


The Mn3O4NPs were prepared using the hydrothermal synthesis method. 22Simply, 4mL0.5M EDTA-2Na aqueous solution was  rstly added into10mL of0.2M KMnO4aqueous solution under stirring, and the color of the solution changed from purple to black-brown immediately when they were mixed together which referred to the formation of a stable complex. Secondly, the mixed solution was transferred into a100mL Te  on cup, and then  lled with ultra-pure water up to about70mL. Then, 2M HNO3was used to adjust the pH of the solution to6.0under vigorous magnetic stirring for10min. The Te  on-lined stainless steel autoclave was sealed and maintained in an oven at180 /C14C for4h and was cooled down to room temperature. The light brown products were obtained by  ltration, washed with ultra-pure water several times followed by ethanol, then dried at60 /C14C for6h. In the following experiments, the nanoparticles were ultrasonically dispersed in ultrapure water to a certain concentration directly before use.


# 2.3Instrumentation


Absorption measurements were performed on a UV-2450spectrophotometer (Shimadzu, Japan). The pH of the solution was detected by a PHS-3D precision pH meter (Shanghai Precision Scienti  c Instruments Co., Ltd., China). The transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images of the Mn3O4NPs were obtained from a Tecnai G2 20 (FEI, USA) operating at200kV, and equipped with an energy dispersive X-ray spectrometer (EDX) for elemental analysis. The scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images were obtained from an S-4800  eld emission scanning electron microscope (Hitachi, Japan) with an accelerating voltage of15kV. The X-ray diffraction (XRD) pattern of the as-prepared products was recorded using an XD-3X-ray diffractometer (PuXi, Beijing, China) under the conditions of nickel  ltered Cu-K a radiation ( l ¼ 0.15406nm) at a current of20mA and a voltage of36kV. The scanning rate was4 /C14min /C0 1in the angular range of10 -70 /C14 (2q ). The Fourier transform infrared (FT-IR) spectrum of the as prepared Mn3O4was obtained using a Tenson27Fourier Transform Infrared spectrometer (Bruker, Germany).


# 2.4General procedure for colorimetric detection and antioxidant activity assessment of phenols


In a typical process, an aqueous solution of the Mn3O4NPs with a concentration of5mg L /C0 1was added to a TMB solution (  nal concentration of10 /C0 4M) in the presence of acetate buffer (pH3.5). The mixture was incubated at35 /C14C for30min. Then the UV-vis spectral measurements and photographs were taken. In order to evaluate the effects of various factors on the oxidaselike activity of the Mn3O4NPs, the relative activity was used as the criterion. The maximum point in each curve was set as100%. Hence, the relative activity was de  ned as the ratio of the absorbance at the target point to the maximum point.

For assessing the antioxidant activities of different phenols using the proposed method, 0.25mL of each10 /C0 4Mphenol was mixed with0.5mL of10 /C0 3M TMB, 0.25mL of100mg L /C0 1

Mn3O4NPs, and4mL of0.2M NaAc buffer (pH3.5) for30min of incubation at35 /C14C. Then the maximum absorbance at655nm was recorded. This TMB method to measure the relative antioxidant activity of phenols was expressed as the inhibitory ratio of the TMB -Mn3O4colorimetric reaction a  er the addition of phenols to the system. The relative absorbance of ( A0 655nm /C0A655nm)/ A0 655nm was used.

For colorimetric detection, the F -C method described by the International Organization for Standardization (ISO) 14502-1was used. 23Brie  y, 5.0mL of10% (v/v) Folin -Ciocalteu's reagent was  rstly mixed with1.0mL of diluted phenol working solution for5min. Then4.0mL of7.5% (w/v) sodium carbonate solution was added and well mixed. The mixture was allowed to stand at room temperature for60min and then the maximum absorbance at765nm ( A765nm) was measured. The relative antioxidant activity of the phenols was expressed as the relative activity. In the colorimetric reaction of different phenols with F -C reagent, the A765nm values were recorded, among which the maximum value was set as100% and the relative activity was de  ned as the ratio of the absorbance at the target value to the maximum value.


# 2.5Procedures for determination of tannic acid in tea samples


For TA determination in tea, three tea samples taken from the local supermarket were chosen. 0.2g of tea sample was added to50mL of ultra-pure water, and the mixture was boiled for10min, naturally cooled to room temperature, then  ltrated through a0.22m m micropore membrane and the  ltrate was collected24and diluted to50mL with ultra-pure water. This solution was further diluted for analysis. Then, under the optimum conditions, 0.5mL of the diluted  ltrate was mixed with0.5mL of10 /C0 3MTMB, 0.25mL of10mg L /C0 1Mn3O4NPs, and3.75mL of0.2M acetate buffer (pH4.0). Then the mixture was incubated at35 /C14C for30min. Finally, the UV-vis absorbance at655nm was detected. The reduction values of the absorbance at655nm ( D A ¼ A0 /C0Ai ) ( A0and Ai were the absorbance without TA and that with a TA concentration of i , respectively) versus the different concentrations of TA were used for the calibration curve for TA. The content of TA in the samples was quanti  ed using the standard curve method.


# 3. Results and discussion



# 3.1Characterization of the Mn3O4NPs


The SEM image (Fig. 1A) shows that the morphology of the Mn3O4NPs was almost octahedral. Though the size distribution of the Mn3O4nano-octahedrons was not uniform, the facets of the octahedrons were smooth and the lengths of the square base sides of the larger nano-octahedrons were about150nm. The TEM image of the individual nano-octahedrons in Fig. S1 (see the ESI † ) also proved the uniform tetragonal shape of the Mn3O4NPs which was consistent with the SEM analysis. The analysis of the EDX spectrum (Fig. S1, ESI † ) con  rmed that the  nely crystallized nano-octahedrons were composed of Mn and O without other impurities.

Fig. 1 (A) SEM image and (B) XRD pattern of the as-obtained Mn3O4NPs.

The XRD pattern of the as-synthesized sample is shown in Fig. 1B. As for the reported results in the literature, 22,25the asobtained product was well crystallized and all diffraction peaks were well assigned to the tetragonal structure of hausmannite Mn3O4except for the very small proportion of peaks for pyrolusite MnO2marked with asterisks.

The FT-IR absorption spectrum of the Mn3O4NPs is shown in Fig. S2 (see the ESI † ). Three signi  cant absorption bands were displayed in the range of400 -650cm /C0 1 . The vibration bands located at628cm /C0 1and520cm /C0 1are characteristic of Mn -O stretching modes in tetrahedral sites and octahedral sites, respectively, and the vibration band located at410cm /C0 1could be assigned to the vibration of manganese species (Mn3+ -O) in the octahedral site of Mn3O4. 26Moreover, the broad absorption bands at3420and3163cm /C0 1could be attributed to the O -H stretching vibrating modes of the absorbed water, and the bands at1638, 1401and1108cm /C0 1should be attributed to the Mn -OH vibration. 27All the characteristic results above indicated that the prepared material was Mn3O4.


# 3.2Characterization of the oxidase-like activity of the Mn3O4NPs


The enzyme-like activity of the Mn3O4NPs was investigated by using TMB as the enzyme substrate. As was expected, the as

prepared Mn3O4NPs could catalyze the oxidation of TMB quickly even in the absence of H2O2to produce a typical blue colour. The oxidative product of TMB showed a maximum absorbance peak at655nm as shown in Fig. 2A, which indicated the efficient catalysis of Mn3O4NPs and that they could act as an oxidase mimic. Also the Mn3O4NPs could catalyze the oxidation reactions of other enzyme substrates without H2O2, such as ABTS and OPD, to produce their respective typical colors, as shown in Fig. 2A. From the curve of the timedependent absorbance changes of TMB at655nm in Fig. 2B, it was found that the rate of the Mn3O4NP-catalyzed reaction of the oxidation of TMB increased steeply within5min and then remained steady. The absorbance slope of the TMB -Mn3O4NP system at655nm was much higher than that of the autoxidation of TMB. These results proved the excellent oxidase-like activity of Mn3O4NPs. A  er the solution was purged with nitrogen, the catalytic reaction rate of TMB oxidation decreased quickly as shown in Fig. 2B, which indicated that the dissolved oxygen was involved in the TMB oxidation. So we presumed that the oxidase mimic activity of the Mn3O4NPs was derived from the catalytic reduction of dissolved oxygen with the Mn3O4NPs.

# 3.3Possible reaction mechanism


To prove the possible reaction mechanism, it is essential to illustrate the production of reactive oxygen species (ROS) from the decomposition of molecular oxygen catalyzed by Mn3O4NPs. Manganese oxides are known to be active catalysts and they also have been applied as oxygen storage components. It has been proved that the oxygen absorption behavior of manganese oxide is superior to that of cerium oxide. 28The Mn3O4NPs possess the oxidation states Mn2+ /Mn3+ , and manganese transfers electrons to O2causing the formation of superoxide anions (O2c /C0 ) 29as in the following reaction (eqn (1)). O2c /C0would be expected to produce H2O2partly by nonenzymatic or SOD-catalyzed dismutation30 (eqn (2)). Furthermore, it was possible that a small quantity of H2O2was decomposed into hydroxyl radicals (OH c ) in the presence of transition metal ions30 (eqn (3)). Then, O2c /C0 /OH c and Mn3+ might further oxidize the enzyme substrate to give the relative color. So, we presume that O2c /C0and a few OH c radicals may be produced in this TMB -Mn3O4NP system.

In order to validate the above presumption, we added radical inhibitors into the system. It is known that t -butyl alcohol is a strong OH c radical scavenger31and p -benzoquinone is able to trap superoxide anions radicals effectively. 32As shown in Fig. S3 (see the ESI † ), the addition of1mM p -benzoquinone obviously inhibited the color reaction, when20mM t -butyl alcohol only had little effect. So from the results we could deduce that O2c /C0radicals were generated mainly during this catalytic reaction as well as a few OH c radicals. The results were well matched with our deductions above, that is, the oxygen free radicals (O2c /C0 /OH c ) were originated from the reduction of dissolved oxygen adsorbed on the Mn3O4NP surface, which further con  rmed that the dissolved oxygen participated in the TMB -Mn3O4NP system. Based on the above discussion, the possible reaction mechanism of the TMB -Mn3O4NP system is summarized in Scheme1.


# 3.4pH and temperature dependence of the Mn3O4NP oxidase-like activity


In order to ensure the reaction proceeded to completion, a30min incubation period was chosen for the following

Scheme1The possible reaction mechanism of the TMB -Mn3O4NP system.

experiments. The catalytic activity of the Mn3O4NPs was similar to the natural enzyme, depending on the pH, temperature and the concentration of the catalyst. We varied the pH from1.0to12.0, the temperature from25to65 /C14C, and the concentration of the Mn3O4NPs from1.0to9.0mg L /C0 1to measure the oxidaselike activity of the Mn3O4NPs. The results in Fig. S4 (see the ESI † ) indicated that the optimal pH and temperature for the Mn3O4NPs were3.5and35 /C14C, respectively. Similar to HRP, the catalytic activity of Mn3O4NPs in acidic medium was much higher than that in neutral or basic solutions, resulting from the acid-promoted colorimetric reaction of TMB. 33So the nature of pH dependent catalytic activity is primarily dependent on the TMB substrate itself rather than the nano-catalyst. The absorbance at655nm increased with the increase of the concentration of the Mn3O4NPs in the system, but in consideration of saving the reagent, we  nally chose5mg L /C0 1as the optimal concentration of the Mn3O4NPs.


# 3.5Stability of the Mn3O4NPs and kinetic analysis


As an inorganic nanomaterial, Mn3O4NPs are considered to be more stable than the natural enzyme, and the stability of the Mn3O4NPs at a wide range of pH values and temperatures was investigated. A  er incubating the Mn3O4NPs at a range of pH values and temperatures for2h, we measured their catalytic activity under the standard conditions. It was found that the relative activity of the Mn3O4NPs still remained above80% over the wide range of pH1to12and temperatures of20to80 /C14C (Fig. S5, ESI † ). So the application of Mn3O4NPs was feasible under harsh conditions due to their robustness. Besides, the catalytic activity study suggested that the batch-to-batch reproducibility among three batches of Mn3O4NPs prepared at different times is satisfactory with a relative standard deviation (RSD) of less than5% (Table S1, ESI † ). A  er one month of storage, the relative catalytic activity of the Mn3O4NPs was maintained above88% (Fig. S6, ESI † ), showing their long-term storage stability. This is because the zeta potential of the Mn3O4suspensions was determined to be about /C0 12.6mV at pH3.5, indicating the negatively charged surface of the Mn3O4suspensions. Hence, the Mn3O4suspensions maintain good stability due to their strong electrostatic repulsion effects.

Fig. 3Steady-state kinetic assay of TMB oxidation with Mn3O4NPs. The velocity ( v ) of the reaction was measured using5mg L /C0 1Mn3O4NPs in pH3.5acetate buffer at35 /C14C.

To determine the steady-state kinetic parameters and obtain the typical Michaelis -Menten curve, a double reciprocal plot between the initial reaction velocity ( v ) and the concentration of TMBis presented under the optimal conditions. The Michaelis -Menten constant ( Km) and maximum reaction ( Vmax) were calculated from the linear  tting in Fig. 3. It is known that the Km value re  ects the affinity of a natural enzyme for a substrate, and the lower the Km value, the higher the affinity between the substrate and enzyme. For the oxidization of TMB by dissolved oxygen, the Km value of the Mn3O4NPs was lower than that of Fe3O4, Co3O4NPs, nanoceria and BSA -MnO2NPs except for noble metal Au@Pt nanorods (Table1). Compared with the non-noble metal NPs, the affinity of the as-prepared Mn3O4NPs for TMB was enhanced by at least35%, which may be due to the special nano-octahedron structure. All the data shows that the Mn3O4NPs are an oxidase mimic with high catalytic activity.


# 3.6Application of the TMB -Mn3O4NP system for the antioxidant activity assessment of phenols and tannic acid detection


Phenolic compounds exist in various plants and foods, and they have been known to have numerous biological effects such as antioxidant activity. Until now, two free radicals have usually been used for assessing antioxidant activity, namely, ABTS + c and2,2-diphenyl-1-picrylhydrazyl (DPPH c ). 35Both methods are based on the free radical scavenging assay, a reaction of an antioxidant with a radical (ABTS + c or DPPH c ), resulting in a decrease in the radical's characteristic absorbance. 36Similarly, when the phenols were added to the TMB -Mn3O4NP system, the TMB + c radical's characteristic absorbance decreased. So it was possible to use the TMB decolorization assay to evaluate the antioxidant activity of phenols.

In this research, under the same reaction conditions, we investigated the inhibition effects of twelve phenols on the TMB -Mn3O4NP system to preliminarily assess their antioxidant abilities, and the inhibition potency results were contrasted with those of the F -C method. The antioxidant tendency results obtained by the proposed method were in agreement with those obtained by the F -C colorimetric method, as shown in Fig. 4. From the results, TA exhibited the most obvious inhibitory effect on the TMB -Mn3O4NP system and the greatest enhancement effect on the F -C reaction, respectively, which indicated the strongest antioxidant capacity of TA with the two different methods. Also, from Fig. 4, we can conclude that the relative antioxidant activities of these phenols greatly increased


Table 1 Comparison of the kinetic parameters of oxidase mimetics<table><thead><tr><th>Catalyst</th><th>K<sub>m</sub> [mM]</th><th>V<sub>max</sub> [10<sup>-8</sup> M s<sup>-1</sup>]</th><th>Ref.</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Fe<sub>3</sub>O<sub>4</sub> NPs</td><td>0.098</td><td>3.44</td><td>1</td></tr><tr><td>Au@Pt nanorods</td><td>0.006–0.013</td><td>2.5–2.62</td><td>6</td></tr><tr><td>BSA–MnO<sub>2</sub> NPs</td><td>0.039</td><td>5.8</td><td>7</td></tr><tr><td>Nanoceria</td><td>0.8–3.8</td><td>30–70</td><td>14</td></tr><tr><td>Co<sub>3</sub>O<sub>4</sub> NPs</td><td>0.051</td><td>3.30</td><td>34</td></tr><tr><td>Mn<sub>3</sub>O<sub>4</sub> NPs</td><td>0.025</td><td>5.07</td><td>This work</td></tr></tbody></table>


Fig. 4Effect of phenols on the TMB -Mn3O4system and their relative activity in the F -C method.

with the increased number of -OH group in these phenols, and the phenols with relatively high antioxidant activity were in the following decreasing order: tannic acid > gallic acid > pyrogallol, which is also identical to the previous results. 37Thus, there is the potential to use the TMB -Mn3O4NP system for the preliminary assessment of phenol antioxidant activity.

The determination of tannic acid was chosen as an example to investigate the possibility of TMB -Mn3O4NP system in real samples. Firstly, the optimum reaction conditions in the presence of1m MTA were studied and the results were obtained as follows: pH4.0 (0.2M acetate buffer), 35 /C14Cand5mgL /C0 1Mn3O4NPs (Fig. S7, ESI † ). Under the optimum conditions, the visual detection of TA was realized, and the calibration data for0.05 -1.4m M was well described by the equation: D A ¼ 0.389c TA + 0.0189 ( R2 ¼ 0.9934, n ¼ 8) (Fig. 5), where D A is the inhibited absorbance intensity when TA is added in the TMB -Mn3O4NP system, and c is the TA concentration ( m M). The limit of detection (LOD) was19nM (3s ). Then, the proposed method was applied to analyze tannic acid in three tea samples. Recovery tests were also carried out on the samples. From the results (Table S2, ESI † ), the recoveries for the spiked tea samples

Fig. 5UV-vis absorption spectra of the TMB -Mn3O4NP system in the presence of different concentrations of tannic acid. Inset: standard calibration curve for the tannic acid assay and the relative photographic images.

ranged from93.4to108.7% at the three spiked concentration levels. These results indicated that the proposed method was satisfactory for real sample analysis.


# Conclusions


In summary, we have successfully synthesized Mn3O4NPs with a simple hydrothermal method and demonstrated their intrinsic oxidase-like activity. The Mn3O4NPs showed a good affinity for TMB due to the small Km value of0.025mM from the typical Michaelis -Menten curve, and the affinity was increased by at least35.9% compared with other non-noble metal NPs oxidase nanozymes. We deduced that the oxygen free radicals (O2c /C0 /OH c ) were originated from the reduction of dissolved oxygen adsorbed on the Mn3O4NP surface. Based on the inhibition of twelve phenols on the TMB -Mn3O4NP system, we developed a simple method to preliminary screen the antioxidant activity of phenols. On this basis, a simple and sensitive colorimetric assay for tannic acid in tea samples was developed and as low as19nM TA could be detected using this method.
